The Slaughterhouse Cases, also known as Ex parte Merryman, were a landmark U.S. Supreme Court case decided in 1873. The cases involved a dispute over the regulation of slaughterhouses in the state of Illinois.

The plaintiffs in the case were the owners of a slaughterhouse in Chicago, who challenged the constitutionality of a law that regulated the sale of meat in the state. The defendants were the state of Illinois and the city of Chicago.

The plaintiffs lived in Chicago, Illinois.

details.expected: a biocidal effect of metals
details.got:
The oligodynamic effect is a phenomenon that describes the antimicrobial or antifungal activity of certain metals, particularly heavy metals, against microorganisms such as bacteria, fungi, and viruses.

More specifically, the oligodynamic effect is characterized by the ability of certain metals, such as copper, silver, and gold, to inhibit the growth or kill microorganisms when they come into contact with them.

The exact mechanism of the oligodynamic effect is not fully understood, but it is thought to involve the interaction of the metal ions with the cell membranes of microorganisms, disrupting their cell structure and function, ultimately leading to their death.

Option C: If A then not B
- If all students passed, then not all students have passed.

Option D: If B then not A
- If not all students passed, then all students have passed.

Now, let's compare the given statement with the options:

- Option A is the most similar to the given statement because it also implies that if all students passed, then all students have passed.

The other options do not have the same implication as the given statement. Option B implies that if not all students passed, then all students have passed, which is not the same as the given statement.

Therefore, the most similar reasoning is Option A: If A then B.

details.expected: Former US President Bill Clinton is introduced to the McGuinness family by Sinn Féin President Gerry Adams
details.got: On 23 March 2017, the headline for this news story is: "23 March 2017: Last updated at 14:55 GMT"

details.expected: Keepers at Chester Zoo are making sure every creature, from the biggest elephant to the smallest beetle, is present and correct as part of their annual animal count.
details.got: Thousands of animals, including endangered species, are counted by the zoo's staff to maintain their license and contribute to global breeding programs. Newsround's Martin visited the zoo to learn about the process.

details.expected: A 19-year-old man has been charged after a cat was killed and others were badly injured in shootings in Surrey.
details.got: Franky Mills, a 23-year-old man from Long Gore in Farncombe, faces eight charges of criminal damage and eight of a firearms offense after shooting eight pets with an air rifle in Cranleigh, Guildford, and Woking in March and April. A five-year-old cat named Ruby was shot in the spine and put down by vets, while another cat in Cranleigh survived but lost her eye. Police have released a summary of the incident and are awaiting his appearance before Guildford magistrates on 9 August.

details.expected: Leicester City striker Gary Taylor-Fletcher has joined Sheffield Wednesday on an initial month-long loan.
details.got: Former Blackpool forward Taylor-Fletcher has signed a new one-year deal with Premier League club Leicester City, joining the Foxes after scoring 33 goals in 23 games for Blackpool last season.

details.expected: An artist's impression of how an expanded primary school in Addlestone will look has been unveiled.
details.got: Surrey County Council will double the size of Sayes Court, a school in Esher, by September 2015 to accommodate an additional 210 places, addressing a £215m funding gap and an "unprecedented demand" on its services.

details.got:
Here is a one-sentence headline summarizing the article: 

Cranleigh, Guildford, and Woking residents were injured in air rifle attacks in March and April, with one cat losing its eye and another cat shot in the spine.

details.expected: Leicester City striker Gary Taylor-Fletcher has joined Sheffield Wednesday on an initial month-long loan.
details.got:

Taylor-Fletcher joins Sheffield Wednesday on loan from Sheffield United.

details.expected: An artist's impression of how an expanded primary school in Addlestone will look has been unveiled.
details.got:
Here is a one-sentence headline summarizing the information: 

Surrey County Council plans to double its school capacity by 2015, with a £215m funding gap to be bridged.
